records.raw_query: paragraph compares WS-DAYS-SINCE-ISSUE to a literal and branches into an OTP consent workflow; a second path force-closes the account after a waiting period measured in working days
records.description: A card issuer must obtain the cardholder's OTP-based consent to activate a card within thirty days of issuance and close the account without cost when consent is not received.

records.raw_query: routine reverses billed charges for a card the customer never requested and computes a penalty amount payable to the person who received the card
records.description: When an unsolicited credit card is issued and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a monetary penalty.

records.raw_query: IF WS-AVAIL-CREDIT negative, transaction still posts over the sanctioned limit without checking any cardholder consent flag
records.description: An over-limit credit card transaction may be permitted only after explicit prior consent from the cardholder, with a facility to enable or disable over-limit usage.

records.raw_query: closure request queue ages entries in working days and accrues a flat per-day penalty of 500 once a deadline passes
records.description: A credit card closure request must be completed within seven working days, with a penalty of five hundred rupees per day of delay.

records.raw_query: batch scan of WS-LAST-TXN-DATE flags card accounts with no activity for over a year and kicks off closure intimation
records.description: A credit card unused for more than one year may be closed after the cardholder is notified and given an opportunity to respond.

records.raw_query: minimum amount due formula folds unpaid interest, levies and taxes into the current cycle so the outstanding balance can never grow month over month
records.description: The minimum amount due must be calculated so unpaid charges, interest, levies and taxes do not cause negative amortisation of the card balance.

records.raw_query: delinquency reporting to credit information companies and penal charge levy are gated on WS-DAYS-PAST-DUE exceeding a small literal
records.description: A card account is treated as past due for credit information reporting and penal charges only when it remains overdue for more than three days.

records.raw_query: interest base computation nets payments, refunds and reversed transactions out of the outstanding before applying the rate
records.description: Finance charges must be applied only to the adjusted outstanding amount after deducting payments, refunds and reversed transactions.

records.raw_query: fee schedule table with effective-dating; changed charge rates apply only prospectively after a one-month notice window
records.description: Changes to credit card charges must be notified at least one month in advance and applied prospectively from their effective date.

records.raw_query: statement generation leaves a minimum fortnight gap between bill dispatch and the date interest starts accruing
records.description: A credit card statement must provide at least a fortnight between dispatch of the bill and the payment due date.

records.raw_query: disputed bill workflow must attach a written explanation with documentary evidence before the charge can be re-billed
records.description: For a disputed credit card transaction, the issuer must provide a written explanation and documentary evidence before re-billing the charge.

records.raw_query: auto-adjustment of refund credit against fresh card spends requires explicit cardholder consent beyond a threshold
records.description: A refund credited to a card account must not be adjusted against subsequent spending beyond the prescribed amount without explicit cardholder consent.

records.raw_query: WS-TOTAL-DUE derived net of credits received during the billing cycle in the statement build paragraph
records.description: The total amount due on a credit card statement is the amount owed after crediting payments and other credits received during the billing cycle.

records.raw_query: complaint aging monitor escalates the case when the response clock crosses a month from lodgement
records.description: A credit card complaint must receive a response within thirty days from the date it is lodged.

records.raw_query: post-settlement job pushes the corrected defaulter status to credit bureaus inside a fixed deadline after dues are cleared
records.description: After a cardholder settles outstanding dues, the issuer must update the corrected default status with credit information companies within the prescribed period.

records.raw_query: issuance flow PERFORMs the KYC-AML verification module as mandated by RBI directions before opening the card account
records.description: Before issuing a credit card or opening the account, the bank must complete customer due diligence under the applicable RBI KYC and anti-money-laundering directions.

records.raw_query: WS-YEARS-SINCE-KYC compared against thresholds that depend on WS-RISK-RATING to schedule customer re-verification
records.description: Periodic KYC updation must occur at risk-based intervals, with more frequent updates for high-risk customers and longer intervals for medium- and low-risk customers.

records.raw_query: loop over partner ownership percentage fields marking any natural person above a cutoff literal as beneficial owner
records.description: Customer due diligence must identify a natural person as beneficial owner when the person's ownership or controlling interest exceeds the prescribed percentage threshold.

records.raw_query: batch uploads amended customer KYC records to the central registry within a days-based SLA of receiving the update
records.description: A regulated entity must upload updated customer KYC information to the Central KYC Records Registry within seven days of receiving the update.

records.raw_query: IF WS-ACTV-FLAG = 'N' AND WS-DAYS-SINCE-ISSUE > 30 MOVE 'Y' TO WS-SEND-OTP-CONSENT
records.description: A card that has not been activated must obtain OTP-based cardholder consent within thirty days of issuance, otherwise the issuer must close the card account.

records.raw_query: grace period of a few days before an account is treated as past due for bureau reporting and penalty purposes
records.description: Credit information reporting and penal charges may begin only after a credit card account has remained past due for more than three days.

records.raw_query: which amount field represents everything the cardholder owes this cycle after netting cycle credits, as opposed to the minimum payment
records.description: Total amount due means the complete amount owed by the cardholder for the billing cycle after netting all credits, distinct from the minimum amount due.

records.raw_query: seven day deadline counted from receiving updated documents, not the seven working day account closure deadline
records.description: A regulated entity must update the Central KYC Records Registry with revised customer information within seven days after receiving that customer information.

records.raw_query: yearly-scale dormancy or refresh check driven by customer risk category rather than card inactivity
records.description: Periodic KYC updation is scheduled according to the customer's risk category, using shorter intervals for high-risk customers rather than card-account inactivity.
